retrieval.query_summary: CADCHF trade analysis for Intraday style, considering SMC/SnD candidates, macro factors, and timeframe alignment.

factors.name: Macro bias alignment
factors.notes: No specific macro data for CAD or CHF, DXY is informational for cross pair.

factors.name: HTF structure aligned OR Setup is a valid Counter-Trend Pullback
factors.notes: Bearish candidates align with overall MN1/W1 bearish trend and H1/M15 bearish structure, indicating a continuation after a D1-H4 retracement. (STYLE-TF-001, STYLE-SETUP-004)

factors.name: MTF (H1/M15) BOS or ChoCH confirmed in trade direction
factors.notes: H1 and M15 show bearish CHoCH and SMS events, confirming bearish momentum.

factors.name: Valid Structural Entry Support
factors.notes: SMC candidates identified valid Order Blocks/FVGs for entry. (STYLE-SETUP-003)

factors.name: Liquidity sweep into entry zone
factors.notes: No liquidity sweep detected into the entry zones of the candidates.

factors.name: Wyckoff phase supports direction
factors.notes: No specific Wyckoff phase identified to support the direction.

factors.name: No high-impact news within 30 minutes
factors.notes: No high-impact news events scheduled within 30 minutes of the analysis timestamp. (MR-REJECT-001, STYLE-AVOID-001)

factors.name: Minimum R:R achievable
factors.notes: The best available candidate (M5 CHOCH+BMS+IDM+RTO) failed to meet the minimum 1:3 R:R for Intraday style, achieving only 1:2.5. (MR-REJECT-010, STYLE-RR-001)

cot_signal.summary: No TFF data available for COT analysis.

raw_response.explainable_reasoning:
The analysis for CADCHF at 2026-05-22T05:04:00Z identifies a 'NO SETUP' condition. The overall market trend on the Monthly (MN1) and Weekly (W1) timeframes is BEARISH. While the Daily (D1) down to H4 timeframes show a bullish retracement, the H1 and M15 timeframes have shifted back to a BEARISH structure, aligning with the higher timeframe bearish bias. This indicates a potential continuation of the overall bearish trend (STYLE-TF-001, STYLE-SETUP-004). The current session is Asian, which is typically disabled for new entries, but allowed for testing as per system rules (MR-REJECT-002, STYLE-SESSION-001, Section E, Rule 10). All news events are low impact, so no high-impact news risk is present (MR-REJECT-001, STYLE-AVOID-001). 

Several live SMC and SnD candidates were identified. The SnD candidates (QMH_KILLER_TYPE2 BULLISH and QML_KILLER_TYPE2 BEARISH) were all invalidated because they lacked Marubozu candle confirmation, which is a mandatory rule for SnD patterns (Section B.2 of the prompt). 

Two live SMC bearish candidates remained: an M5 CHOCH+BMS+IDM+RTO pattern with an Order Block between 0.57437 and 0.57455, and an M1 SH+BMS+IDM+FVG+RTO pattern with an Order Block between 0.5723 and 0.57235. Both candidates align with the overall bearish trend and the immediate H1/M15 bearish structure. However, upon calculating the Risk:Reward (R:R) ratio for the best available candidate (M5 CHOCH+BMS+IDM+RTO), it was found to be approximately 1:2.5 (entry 0.57446, SL 0.574568, TP 0.57419). This fails to meet the minimum 1:3 R:R requirement for the 'Intraday' trading style, as per rule STYLE-RR-001. The user's preference for a minimum R:R of 2.0 is overridden by the institutional rulebook's stricter requirement for the Intraday style (Section F, Rule 3).

Furthermore, the confluence score for the best candidate was 4, which is below the minimum threshold of 5 for a 'B' grade (MR-REJECT-009). The failure to meet the mandatory minimum R:R requirement (MR-REJECT-010), combined with the invalidation of all SnD candidates due to missing Marubozu, leads to a rejection of all potential setups. Therefore, the system will not proceed with any trade execution and will remain patient, waiting for a higher-quality setup that meets all mandatory criteria (MR-PHIL-005, Section B.4 of the prompt).

htf_bias.notes: Overall trend is bearish on MN1 and W1 timeframes. D1 to H4 timeframes show a bullish retracement within this larger bearish trend.

mtf_bias.notes: H1 and M15 timeframes are currently bearish, aligning with the overall HTF bearish trend.
